Campos is a football UI library for humans and agents.

Primary architecture docs:
- docs/architecture-decision.md
- docs/README.md

Active workspace packages:
- packages/react: primary runtime package for chart components such as <ShotMap />
- packages/adapters: provider normalization such as fromOpta.shots()
- packages/schema: canonical football types and schema helpers
- packages/stadia: pitch and goal primitives for football surfaces
- packages/static: server-side SVG/PNG export

Implementation conventions:
- Prefer canonical entities from @withqwerty/campos-schema over provider-shaped objects.
- Use adapters for raw data normalization instead of embedding provider logic in renderers.
- Keep @withqwerty/campos-react task-shaped and zero-config friendly.
- Treat @withqwerty/campos-react as the primary consumer entry point for charts.

Current workspace shape:
- React-first chart package
- active adapter package
- shared schema package
- shared Stadia surface primitives
- static export package
- Astro demo/docs site at https://campos.withqwerty.com (separate repo)

Agent-facing registry:
- chart and adapter catalogue: https://campos.withqwerty.com/r/registry.json
- per-chart metadata: https://campos.withqwerty.com/r/charts/<Name>.json
- per-adapter metadata: https://campos.withqwerty.com/r/adapters/<provider>.json
- provider × chart capability matrix: https://campos.withqwerty.com/r/adapter-matrix.json
- recipes per chart: https://campos.withqwerty.com/r/recipes/<Name>.json
- schemaVersion: 1, updated on every site deploy
